item.title: Talos Energy hit by $145 million impairment charge, Q1 losses deepen
item.description: The company produced 88,800 barrels of oil equivalent per day of oil and gas in the first quarter, exceeding its guidance

item.title: Australia gas export tax inquiry set to issue recommendations
item.description: Operators say that a proposed gas export tax would make new projects 'uninvestible'

item.title: Petronas launches drillship re-tender for ultra-deepwater wildcat
item.description: Malaysian operator hopes to spud Redha-1 exploration well offshore Indonesia by October

item.title: Australia and Japan ink energy security cooperation agreement
item.description: The two countries signed a joint statement in “a commitment to mutual energy security and to rules-based and market-oriented trade and investment”

item.title: Southeast Asia gas development advances with subsea pipelaying
item.description: First gas from Malampaya phase four project expected in fourth quarter of 2026

item.title: QatarEnergy begins process to repair two LNG mega-trains hit in Iran attack
item.description: QatarEnergy said March attacks damaged two liquefaction trains with combined production capacity of 12.8 million tonnes per annum

item.title: US official defends BOEM-BSEE merger as ‘key component’ of offshore production
item.description: Department of the Interior’s Jacob Tyner says reunification of agencies split after 2010 BP spill will be ‘multi-stage process’

item.title: Senegal starts building pipeline and power plant taking gas from BP asset
item.description: Gas will flow to domestic market from Greater Tortue Ahmeyim (GTA) field, according to partner Kosmos Energy

item.title: Subsea7 awards pre-commissioning job for ultra-deepwater project
item.description: EnerMech secured subsea contract for Trion project offshore Mexico

item.title: Norway drilling technology specialist acquired by US energy services player
item.description: Expro to buy offshore drilling services supplier Enhanced Drilling for $215 million

item.title: US shale player sees ‘signal’ needed to begin growing production
item.description: Diamondback Energy to add more barrels ‘immediately’, CEO says in shareholder letter

item.title: Subsea tie-back boom at risk from supply chain shortages, experts warn
item.description: New vessels and equipment are needed to meet demand for what is quickly becoming a standard offshore development path

item.title: Nova Scotia premier makes another personal pitch for offshore energy investments
item.description: Province's leader Tim Houston eager to attract offshore energy explorers to eastern Candian province

item.title: Norway launches 2026 offshore licensing round
item.description: Total of 70 exploration blocks available for competitive bidding

item.title: BP hints at deploying early production system at mega Brazil pre-salt find
item.description: UK supermajor fast-tracking development at Bumerangue discovery in Santos basin

item.title: NewMed chief executive resigns to pursue 'opportunities on global stage'
item.description: Yossi Abu has helmed Israel's biggest E&P player since 2011, after a chance meeting with Gideon Tadmor

item.title: Amid energy market upheaval, advances in technology have rarely been more important
item.description: Geopolitical upheaval underscores need for new means and methods to find, develop and secure sources of today's energy

item.title: Equinor awards FEED contract and starts new production
item.description: FEED awarded for Atlantis project while previous discovery Eirin is brought on stream

item.title: Ineos Energy and Shell target US Gulf exploration
item.description: New agreement is part of Ineos Energy's strategy to build global portfolio

item.title: TotalEnergies and Jera Nex BP-backed plan would slow German offshore wind growth
item.description: TotalEnergies offshore wind chief claims benefits for network operators, supply chain and taxpayers if proposals to lessen wake impacts are adopted

item.title: How Europe's biggest blackout helped turn role of renewables on its head
item.description: Some people blamed Spain's high renewables penetration for last year's blackout, but regulatory changes will now allow wind and solar to help stabilise voltage. Industry leaders say more can be done

item.title: Ineos to site massive $1.7bn blue methanol plant at chemicals complex in Texas
item.description: The British company will become a shareholder and anchor customer of Sandpiper Chemicals, which plans to build a 1.1 million tonnes-a-year methanol facility

item.title: Progress for new offshore CCS project in Norway
item.description: Two contractors hired to help develop the Vaar-operated Trudvang CCS project

item.title: Progress for new offshore carbon capture project in Norway
item.description: Two contractors hired to help develop the Vaar-operated Trudvang CCS project

item.title: Fresh drone strike at UAE oil facility triggers 'large' fire, three injured
item.description: Adnoc has been using Fujairah port to avoid Strait of Hormuz, where shipping is disrupted by Middle East conflict

item.title: Long-awaited recovery for seismic sector could be over
item.description: TGS chief executive Kristian Johansen explains that reserves replacement is back in focus

item.title: TGS: Long wait for seismic sector recovery could be over
item.description: TGS chief executive Kristian Johansen says that reserves replacement is back in focus

item.title: Transocean still in the red, CEO sees brighter times ahead
item.description: Rig owner’s Q1 revenue boost insufficient to turn a profit

item.title: Adnoc subsidiary completes acquisition of leading oilfield services player
item.description: UAE-based drilling giant will expand its rig fleet to 170 units, the largest in the Middle East, following the acquisition

item.title: Samsung scoops $330 million prize for Malaysia's first LNG FSRU project
item.description: MISC will own and operate key liquefied natural gas import facility for Petronas Gas
